gloss.en: the way parts are arranged together

words.example: The design is based on four configurations of squares.

gloss.en: to keep within limits or a small space

words.example: The work will not be confined to the Glasgow area.

gloss.en: a statement showing something is true or definite

words.example: I'm still waiting for confirmation of the test results.

gloss.en: to face a problem that must be dealt with

words.example: What is to be done about the problems confronting the country?

gloss.en: an angry conflict between people who disagree

words.example: She wanted to avoid another confrontation with her father.

gloss.en: to tell someone you are pleased about their success

words.example: I congratulated them all on their results.

gloss.en: a group gathered in church for a service

words.example: The congregation stood to sing the hymn.

gloss.en: relating to a congress, especially the US Congress

words.example: They set up a congressional committee.

gloss.en: to take control of a place by force

words.example: The Normans conquered England in 1066.

gloss.en: your sense of what is right or wrong

words.example: She had a clear conscience about her decision.

gloss.en: the state of being awake and aware

words.example: I can't remember any more—I must have lost consciousness.

gloss.en: following one after another without a break

words.example: She was absent for nine consecutive days.

gloss.en: an opinion that everyone in a group agrees with

words.example: She is skilled at achieving consensus on sensitive issues.

gloss.en: permission to do something

words.example: The written consent of a parent is required.

gloss.en: to protect something from harm or waste

words.example: There are new laws to conserve wildlife in the area.

gloss.en: the quality of always behaving the same way

words.example: She has played with great consistency all season.

gloss.en: to make something stronger and more secure

words.example: He has consolidated his position as the leading director.

gloss.en: a district that elects its own representative

words.example: Unemployment is high in her constituency.

gloss.en: to be considered to be something

words.example: Does such an activity constitute a criminal offence?

gloss.en: the basic laws by which a country is governed

words.example: You have the right to vote under the constitution.

gloss.en: relating to or allowed by a constitution

words.example: The court ruled that the new law was constitutional.

gloss.en: something that limits your freedom to act

words.example: We have to work within constraints of time and money.

gloss.en: the act of discussing something before deciding

words.example: The decision was made after consultation with staff.

gloss.en: to think carefully about doing something

words.example: You're too young to be contemplating retirement.

gloss.en: a feeling that someone deserves no respect

words.example: She looked at him with contempt.

gloss.en: to argue that something is true

words.example: I would contend that the minister's thinking is flawed.

gloss.en: a person with a chance of winning

words.example: She is a strong contender for the gold medal.

gloss.en: happy and satisfied with what you have

words.example: He seemed more content, less bitter.

gloss.en: anger or disagreement between people

words.example: One area of contention is the availability of nursery care.

gloss.en: repeatedly and often in an annoying way

words.example: They argue continually about money.

gloss.en: a person or firm hired to do work

words.example: We hired a building contractor to do the work.

gloss.en: a lack of agreement between facts or ideas

words.example: There is a contradiction between the two sets of figures.

gloss.en: opposite in nature or direction

words.example: In the end the contrary was proved true.

gloss.en: a person who writes for or supplies something

words.example: She is a regular contributor to this magazine.

gloss.en: the act of changing something into another form

words.example: Their business is the conversion of farm buildings into homes.

gloss.en: to declare someone guilty in court

words.example: He was convicted of fraud.

gloss.en: the act of finding someone guilty in court

words.example: He plans to appeal against his conviction.

gloss.en: to work together to achieve something

words.example: The two groups agreed to cooperate with each other.

gloss.en: working together towards a shared aim

words.example: Cooperative activity is essential to community work.

gloss.en: to organize parts of an activity to work well

words.example: They appointed a manager to coordinate the work.

gloss.en: the act of making parts work together well

words.example: The aim was to improve the coordination of services.

gloss.en: a person who organizes an activity and its people

words.example: The campaign needs an effective coordinator.

gloss.en: a police officer

words.example: Somebody call the cops!

gloss.en: a soft red-brown metal used in wires and coins

words.example: They opened a new copper mine.

gloss.en: the legal right to publish or use a work

words.example: Copyright expires seventy years after the author dies.

gloss.en: a change that makes something more accurate

words.example: I've made a few small corrections to your report.

gloss.en: to be closely connected and affect each other

words.example: The figures do not seem to correlate.

gloss.en: a connection where one thing changes with another

words.example: There is a direct correlation between sun and skin cancer.

gloss.en: to be the same as or match something

words.example: Your account and hers do not correspond.

gloss.en: the letters and emails a person sends and receives

words.example: She keeps all her personal correspondence in a drawer.

gloss.en: a person who reports news for the media

words.example: She's the BBC's political correspondent.

gloss.en: matching or connected with something mentioned

words.example: A change in money supply brings a corresponding change in spending.

gloss.en: using power dishonestly for personal gain

words.example: It was the only way to overthrow a corrupt regime.

gloss.en: dishonest behaviour by people in authority

words.example: There were allegations of bribery and corruption.

gloss.en: costing a lot of money

words.example: Buying new furniture may prove too costly.

gloss.en: a member of a city or county council

words.example: Councillor Ann Jones spoke at the meeting.

gloss.en: professional advice about a problem

words.example: The couple decided to go for relationship counselling.

gloss.en: a trained person who advises people with problems

words.example: I went to see a debt counsellor for advice.

gloss.en: a flat surface where goods are sold in a shop

words.example: I asked the woman behind the counter for help.

gloss.en: someone with the same role elsewhere

words.example: The Foreign Minister held talks with his Chinese counterpart.

gloss.en: too many to be counted

words.example: I've warned her countless times.

gloss.en: a sudden, often violent, change of government

words.example: He seized power in a military coup in 2008.

gloss.en: polite behaviour that shows respect

words.example: I was treated with the utmost courtesy by the staff.

gloss.en: a skill of making things by hand

words.example: She enjoys traditional crafts like basket-weaving.

gloss.en: to move on your hands and knees

words.example: Our baby is just starting to crawl.

gloss.en: a person who has made or invented something

words.example: Walt Disney was the creator of Mickey Mouse.

gloss.en: the quality that makes people trust someone

words.example: The scandal caused him to lose all credibility.

gloss.en: that can be believed or trusted

words.example: She gave a credible explanation for the delay.

gloss.en: to move slowly and quietly to avoid notice

words.example: I crept up the stairs, trying not to wake my parents.

gloss.en: a written piece of detailed criticism

words.example: She wrote a critique of Freud's theories.

gloss.en: a circular gold headpiece worn by a monarch

words.example: The crown was placed upon the new monarch's head.

gloss.en: in a natural state, not yet processed

words.example: The price of crude oil rose sharply.

gloss.en: to press something hard so it loses shape

words.example: The car was completely crushed under the truck.

gloss.en: a small solid piece of a substance with even sides

words.example: You can see tiny salt crystals on the surface.

gloss.en: very popular with a particular group
gloss.zh: 狂热崇拜的；小众热门的

words.example: The film became a cult classic among students.

gloss.en: to prepare and use land for growing crops

words.example: The land around here has never been cultivated.

gloss.en: a strong desire to know about something

words.example: Children show curiosity about everything.

gloss.en: the legal right to care for someone

words.example: Who will have custody of the children?

gloss.en: an article cut from a newspaper and kept

words.example: She keeps newspaper cuttings about her career.

gloss.en: believing people act only for selfish reasons
gloss.zh: 愤世嫉俗的；玩世不恭的

words.example: He gave a cynical smile and walked away.

gloss.en: a barrier built across a river to hold water

words.example: They built a huge dam across the river.

gloss.en: causing harm or a bad effect

words.example: The decision had damaging consequences for the firm.

gloss.en: the time when light first appears in the sky

words.example: They start work at dawn.

gloss.en: scattered pieces left after destruction

words.example: Teams are still clearing the debris from the crash.

gloss.en: a performer's first public appearance

words.example: He will make his debut for the first team this week.

gloss.en: the process of deciding about important things

words.example: She has responsibility for decision-making in the team.

gloss.en: very important for the final result

words.example: Her support was a decisive factor in our success.

gloss.en: an official or formal public statement

words.example: The leaders agreed to sign a declaration of peace.

gloss.en: working hard at something you care about

words.example: She is a dedicated teacher who loves her job.

gloss.en: the hard work and effort you give something

words.example: Success requires hard work and dedication.

gloss.en: a thing someone does, good or bad

words.example: It's a stirring tale of heroic deeds.

gloss.en: to consider or regard in a certain way

words.example: The evening was deemed a great success.

gloss.en: the setting used when no choice is made

words.example: The default is fifty lines.

gloss.en: a fault that makes something imperfect

words.example: There was a defect in the glass.

gloss.en: protecting against attack

words.example: As a defensive measure he built coastal forts.

gloss.en: a lack of something that is essential

words.example: Vitamin deficiency in the diet can cause illness.

gloss.en: the amount by which spending exceeds income

words.example: The country has a large trade deficit.

gloss.en: to refuse to obey a person or rule

words.example: I wouldn't have dared to defy my teachers.

gloss.en: a person chosen to represent a group

words.example: Congress delegates rejected the proposals.

gloss.en: a group representing an organization or country

words.example: The Dutch delegation arrived at the United Nations.

gloss.en: easily damaged or broken

words.example: She collects delicate china teacups.

gloss.en: an evil spirit

words.example: The people believed the girl was possessed by demons.

gloss.en: a statement that something is not true

words.example: The court ignored the prisoner's repeated denials.

gloss.en: to strongly criticize something as wrong

words.example: She publicly denounced the government's handling of the crisis.

gloss.en: packed closely with little space between

words.example: They walked through a dense forest.

gloss.en: the degree to which something is packed together

words.example: The population density in this city is very high.

gloss.en: the state of needing help or support to survive

words.example: Our relationship was based on mutual dependence.

gloss.en: to show someone or something in a picture

words.example: The painting depicts a quiet country scene.

gloss.en: to move forces into position for action

words.example: Two thousand troops were deployed in the area.

gloss.en: the act of moving forces into position

words.example: They agreed on the deployment of peacekeeping forces.

gloss.en: a first part-payment of a larger sum

words.example: They normally ask you to pay a deposit.

gloss.en: to prevent someone from having something

words.example: The war deprived many children of an education.

gloss.en: the second most important person who can act for the leader

words.example: I'm acting as deputy till the manager returns.

gloss.en: to come or go down to a lower level

words.example: The plane began to descend.

gloss.en: the action of coming or going down

words.example: The plane began its descent to Heathrow.

gloss.en: to officially give something a name or status

words.example: This area has been designated a National Park.

gloss.en: worth having or doing

words.example: Good communication is a desirable quality in a secretary.

gloss.en: the main computer screen showing program icons

words.example: He saved the file to his desktop.

gloss.en: causing destruction or damage

words.example: The war showed the destructive power of modern weapons.

gloss.en: to officially keep someone from leaving

words.example: One man has been detained for questioning.
